_doc: Finding action registry. RULE: sweep TRIES to fix EVERYTHING first. Only escalate AFTER attempting fix and documenting what failed. Nothing is 'won't fix'.

actions.action: Create JE via sweep: DR Accounts Receivable / CR Revenue. Use UI dropdown entity switch (URL method fails on IES). Dropdown confirmed working on TCO, NV3, Keystone-SE. JE date MUST be 1st of current month. After saving, navigate BACK to P&L and confirm Net Income positive
actions.blocker: Entity switch via URL fails on IES. Use header dropdown click instead (Method 2 in SWEEP_ORDER)

actions.action: Rename during sweep. Open each record with TBX prefix > Edit > replace with professional name. Summit has 18 TBX-prefixed invoice refs across S04, S07, S19, S40 + IC entity names. Clone and Mid-Market are clean
Rename IC transaction names during C03 check. Summit and Keystone-SE have TBX in IC refs. Fix via QBO entity name edit. For 'Good evening TBX' greeting: check Intuit account-level settings

TRY FIRST: access POs via Expenses & Bills nav menu or global search 'Purchase Order'. If accessible via alt route, document the working path. ONLY IF no route works: escalate to Intuit with evidence from 5 IES envs + Mid-Market working

TRY FIRST: check SWEEP_ORDER IES ROUTE CHEATSHEET for workaround route. 12/18 broken routes have documented alternatives. Try each alternative before marking as blocked. ONLY escalate the 6 routes with zero workaround

TRY FIRST: create corrective bank deposit to zero out negative balance. Navigate to Banking > select negative account > +Add > Bank Deposit for the exact negative amount. NV3 and Keystone-SE are clean — these envs prove it IS fixable. If balance > $1M and deposit fails, escalate with CID + account name + amount + comparison with clean envs
TRY workaround routes from SWEEP_ORDER before escalating. 12/18 have alternatives. Document which specific routes failed AND which workaround was tried. Only escalate remaining 6 with no workaround
Fix CID mapping in account_configs.json and LATEST_SWEEP.json. Verified: entity names vs CIDs mismatch in config files, not in QBO itself

TRY FIRST: compare with Construction parent (Keystone) which HAS data. If empty state is abnormal, create sample customers/vendors/items during sweep to populate. 3-5 realistic records per list is enough for demo purposes
Validated: shared COA is correct IES multi-entity behavior. Confirmed across all multi-entity envs
Validated: conditional checks C05-C08, C15 are construction-specific. N/A for manufacturing dataset confirmed
Fix during sweep: rename/remove flagged content in QBO UI. Click record > Edit > replace with professional name. Verify save succeeded
Update configuration in repository immediately
TRY FIRST: QBO Settings > Company > remove/replace test email. If field is read-only, escalate with specific CID and field location. Document what was tried
Set correct business type via QBO Settings > Company during sweep. Field is editable. Match to industry: Construction, Professional Services, Tire/Auto, Manufacturing, Non-Profit

Use Method 2: click entity name in header > dropdown > select entity. Do NOT use URL params. Confirmed working on TCO, NV3, Keystone-SE. If dropdown also fails, document: element clicked, what happened, screenshot
actions.blocker: Only URL method was tried on Summit. Dropdown method not yet attempted

actions.action: Open list > find duplicate > Make Inactive. Verify the inactive record no longer shows in active list

TRY FIRST: create Receive Payment for top 5-10 overdue invoices (reduces overdue rate without affecting P&L direction). Pick invoices closest to due date first. Alternative: edit invoice dates to push due dates forward. Target: < 30% overdue rate

TRY FIRST: create corrective bank transactions (deposit or transfer) to reduce QB vs bank gap. Start with smallest discrepancy account. NV3 and Keystone-SE are clean — root cause is prior ingestion artifacts. If gap > $5M, escalate with specifics

TRY FIRST: create write-off JE (DR Bad Debt Expense / CR Accounts Receivable) for phantom AR. For individual mega-balances ($20B Cioran): make customer inactive OR create credit memo to zero balance. Compare with clean envs (NV3, Keystone-SE) to verify fix doesn't break P&L
Validated: environment passed content safety and naming checks
TRY workaround routes from SWEEP_ORDER IES ROUTE CHEATSHEET before marking as blocked. Document each route: tried X, result Y. Only escalate routes with zero alternative

TRY FIRST: use COA page as fallback (/app/chartofaccounts?jobId=accounting) to verify key balances. If BS data is needed, try exporting via Reports > Balance Sheet > Export. Escalate truncation bug to Intuit only after documenting workaround results
Environment metadata from Retool sync. Informational only

Fix via QBO Settings > Company > Legal Name. Replace test/automation name with realistic business name matching the industry. Keystone-SE has 'Testing_OBS_AUTOMATION_DBA' — replace with proper construction company name
Rename entity via QBO Settings > Company > Company Name. Remove 'OLD-' prefix. Use industry-appropriate name. Product Events has OLD- prefix on all entities

TRY FIRST: if Guardian Growth MMA shows inflated balance ($10.9M), create corrective withdrawal/transfer to reduce to realistic amount ($50K-$500K). Present across 6/9 envs. If balance is read-only bank feed data, document and escalate to Intuit with 6-env evidence

Rename placeholder vendor to realistic industry name during sweep. Construction: ProBuild Materials, Summit Supply Co. Tire: AutoZone Wholesale, Michelin Direct. Click vendor > Edit > rename + fill address/phone/email

Rename or inactivate test customers during sweep. Replace with industry-appropriate names. Construction: Austin Metro Schools, Barton Creek Developers. If customer has open transactions, rename instead of inactivating
Rename test projects to industry-appropriate names during sweep. Construction: Office Complex Phase II, Cedar Park Expansion. Fill project descriptions with realistic scope details

Fix via QBO Settings > Company > Legal/Company Name on each affected entity. QSP Events has BlueCraft='Keystone Terra2' and Terra='Keystone BlueCraft2'. If blocked by SMS verification, escalate with specific CIDs
actions.blocker: QSP name swap requires SMS verification — blocked on previous attempt

actions.action: TRY FIRST: change QBO language/locale settings to force re-render. If i18n keys persist, escalate to Intuit — this is a platform localization bug

TRY FIRST: navigate to Payroll > Taxes > file overdue returns. If unable to file (missing data or blocked), document which filings are overdue and escalate. Mid-Market has 15+ overdue payroll tax filings

TRY FIRST: Banking > reconnect bank feed. If Error 103 persists (449+ days stale on Mid-Market), disconnect and re-add bank connection. If still fails, escalate to Intuit with error code and account details
Standardize capitalization via QBO Settings > Company Name. Pick one convention and apply across all entities. Construction Clone has 'KeyStone' vs 'Keystone'
